ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO BIND PLAINTIFFS TO THEIR ORIGINAL DISCOVERY RESPONSES REGARDING WITNESS IDENTIFICATION (C.C.P. 2030.310 (B)). HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY INC.'S MOTION TO BIND PLAINTIFFS TO THEIR ORIGINAL DISCOVERY RESPONSES REGARDING WITNESS IDENTIFICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF'S EARLIER OMISSION OF A WITNESS WAS WILLFUL AND THAT NO ALTERNATIVE REMEDY EXISTS. PLAINTIFFS IDENTIFIED THE WITNESS PRIOR TO THE DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE, AND DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW WHY A CONTINUANCE WOULD NOT BE A SUFFICIENT REMEDY. DEFENDANT'S AND PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against James A Nelson Co Inc
Off Calendar Apr-12-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Matter called with Line 3, Case No. CGC-10-275596 (McClain et al vs. Asbestos Defendants).

APPEARANCES:

Gardiner McKleroy, Esq. of WILSON ELSER (415.433.0990), appearing for moving party Century Indemnity Company,
James Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON PURCELL (415.493.3531), appearing for opposing party / plaintiff Cornelius Murphy.

RULING: Century Indemnity Company's Motion For An Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against James A. Nelson Company, Inc.

Matter called, argument held. At the conclusion or argument, the Court granted Century Indemnity Company's Motion For An Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against James A. Nelson Company, Inc. with a modification with regard to the default as to the extent that it affects Century Indemnity Company and a new liability it may have. Counsel were directed to confer regarding the proposed form of order and present it to the Court for signature. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Court Reporter: Nicole Hatler, CSR #13730 of AikenWelch. (503/CML)

(Off the record, the Court received and signed the proposed order in the McClain case only. See scanned order filed this date for full text.)

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kyle J. Clawson of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company, the insurer of defendant Associated Insulation of California, Inc., a suspended corporation
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Frances Murphy, as Wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-in-Interest to Cornelius Murphy, et al.

RULING:
FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

Prior to the hearing the Court granted parties' request to also argue the same motions in the Kell (#275638), Lee (#275898), and Kase (#275958) cases. Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as to the Murphy case: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The amended default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on March 19, 2012 and the amended default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on October 21, 2014 are set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon , CSR#6840.

Kimberly Chu, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, FRANCES MURPHY, as wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-in Interest to CORNELIUS MURPHY, SR., deceased; and CORNELIUS MURPHY, JR., THERESA MURPHY, COLLEEN CHASE, SEILA BASS, as Legal Heirs of CORNELIUS MURPHY, SR., deceased ("plaintiffs").
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY in the amount of $889,350.00 in Economic Damages, $1,800,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $2,689,350.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA in the amount of $889,350.00 in Economic Damages, $1,800,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $2,689,350.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO GRAVEL CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Stephen E. Carlson of Imai Tadlock Keeney & Cordery LLP (675.7000) for defendant and moving party; Oren Noah of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied as to issues 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6 (negligence - survival and wrongful death, strict liability - survival and wrongful death, and loss of consortium), granted as to issue 7 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). As to issues 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Cantley's declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Murphy was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Gold Bondjoint compound and Monokote fireproofing) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Cantley's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' amended responses to defendant's special interrogatories and Mr. Ay's declaration are sustained. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs represent that they will dismiss their false representation cause of action. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of Aiken Welch, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Annie C. Wu, Esq., of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Cornelius Murphy, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages are denied. As to summary judgment, Mr. Shortall's deposition testimony and declaration and defendant's responses to standard interrogatories (Exhibit JJ) create a triable issue whether Mr. Murphy was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Shortall's declaration are overruled. As to summary adjudication, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to contention interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)
